title: Trump Claims Iran Asked for Ceasefire; Iran Flatly Denies — Competing Narratives on Day 33
detail: President Trump publicly stated on April 1 that Iran's president had contacted him asking for a ceasefire, describing Iran as 'begging to make a deal.' Iran's Foreign Ministry immediately called Trump's claim 'false and baseless,' with spokesman Esmaeil Baghaei saying no such request had been made. Iran's FM Abbas Araghchi clarified on state television that Iran is not seeking a 'ceasefire' but a permanent and comprehensive end to the war — a distinction Tehran considers fundamental. US intelligence reportedly assessed Iran currently believes it holds a strong enough position to resist negotiations. Back channels remain active via Pakistan, Egypt, and Turkey. Vice President Vance was reportedly in contact with mediators as recently as April 1. The Kharazi strike earlier in the day disrupted the Pakistan diplomatic channel, further complicating talks.

title: Iran's FM Presents 5 Conditions for Permanent War End — Rejects 'Ceasefire' Framing
detail: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi publicly laid out Tehran's five conditions for a permanent end to the war on April 1: (1) Full and immediate halt to all US and Israeli strikes on Iranian territory; (2) Verified and legally binding guarantees Iran will not be attacked again; (3) Payment of war reparations; (4) Ceasefire across all fronts — including proxy groups (Hezbollah, Houthis, PMF); and (5) Recognition of Iran's claimed sovereignty rights over the Strait of Hormuz. Araghchi rejected the word 'ceasefire' as implying a temporary pause rather than a permanent resolution, stating Iran's goal is 'a comprehensive and permanent end to the war with guarantees.' The Hill and The Jerusalem Post reported US intel assessed Iran 'unwilling to negotiate' on these terms as presented.

title: IDF Strike Wounds Former Iranian FM Kharazi in Tehran; His Wife Killed — Pakistan Diplomatic Channel Disrupted
detail: Former Iranian Foreign Minister and senior diplomat Kamal Kharazi was seriously wounded when a strike hit his home in Tehran on April 1. His wife was killed in the attack. Kharazi had been serving as a key back-channel contact between Iran and Pakistan's facilitation team — his injury severely disrupted the Pakistan-mediated diplomatic track that had been the primary conduit for US-Iran communications. Iranian officials condemned the strike as a deliberate attempt to destroy diplomacy. Kharazi, who served as FM under Khatami (1997–2005), has remained an influential informal advisor and was among the senior figures managing indirect US-Iran contact. The Intercept had earlier reported allegations of a Pentagon casualty cover-up, with approximately 750 total US casualties alleged — the Pentagon denied the figures.

media.caption: Former Iranian Foreign Minister Kharazi wounded in Tehran airstrike; his wife killed — Pakistan diplomatic channel disrupted

title: Iran Launches New Missile Wave at Israel After Trump's 'Destroyed Military' Statement
detail: Iran launched a new wave of ballistic missiles and drones targeting Israel on April 1 in direct response to Trump's statement that the US had 'destroyed the Iranian military' and was near war objectives. Israeli air defenses intercepted the majority of projectiles; some debris fell in populated areas. The launch, while significantly smaller in scale than early-conflict barrages (launch rate is down ~90% from Day 1), demonstrated Iran's continued missile stockpile despite weeks of strikes on production facilities. The IRGC statement accompanying the launch said Iran was retaliating for Trump's 'victory declaration' and his claim the military had been destroyed. CENTCOM confirmed the wave and noted Iran's pre-stockpiled missiles continue to penetrate defenses despite production being effectively destroyed.

title: Tanker Hit by Projectile Off Doha, Qatar — British Maritime Security Agency Confirms
detail: A tanker was struck by a projectile off the coast of Doha, Qatar on April 1, according to a confirmation from the British Maritime Trade Operations (BMTO) security agency. Damage was reported to the vessel; no casualties were immediately confirmed. The attack is consistent with Iran's ongoing pattern of maritime strikes targeting Gulf energy infrastructure and shipping. Qatar hosts the Al Udeid Air Base — the largest US air base in the region — and has hosted forward US air operations since Day 1. The attack raised fresh concerns about Qatar LNG export operations, which remain under force majeure since Day 2 of the conflict.

title: IEA Chief: April Oil Supply Crunch Will Be 'Much Worse Than March' — $200/bbl Risk
detail: IEA Executive Director Fatih Birol warned on April 1 that April's oil supply crunch will be 'much worse than March' as strategic petroleum reserve releases and exempted Russian/Iranian oil flows approach exhaustion by mid-April. The 'April oil cliff' described by multiple analysts refers to the expiration of approximately 400 million barrels in cumulative SPR releases globally. Axios reported Macquarie estimates a 40% probability of Brent reaching $200/barrel; Eurasia Group gives a 55% probability the war continues through May. CNBC reported oil supply impact is now the single largest shock in modern history as a share of global supply. The Trump hard deadline for full Hormuz reopening remains April 6. Brent crude was trading around $110/bbl as of April 1 with continued volatility.

title: UK Hosts 35-Nation Emergency Conference on Hormuz Reopening; China-Pakistan 5-Point Plan Tabled
detail: Britain convened approximately 35 countries in London on April 1 to discuss the Strait of Hormuz crisis and potential diplomatic pathways to reopening. China and Pakistan tabled a joint 5-point plan including a ceasefire, Hormuz reopening, and international guarantees — the most substantive multilateral proposal since the conflict began. The April 6 Trump deadline for full Hormuz reopening remained in force. Pakistan PM Sharif expressed confidence in Islamabad's mediation track despite the Kharazi strike disruption. Turkey and Egypt continued bilateral outreach to Tehran. Gulf states — already facing severe economic damage from the blockade — pressed for the fastest possible resolution.
